brief_summary_anonymized: Anti-corruption authority wants young Saudis to engage with reporting platforms without fear or cynicism.
strategic_insight: Young Saudis don't lack integrity; they lack confidence the system listens. The campaign earns their trust before asking for their report.
execution_summary: First-person voice from real-feeling Saudi voices. Brand demonstrates listening, not asking. 'You speak, we follow' — not 'report and we punish.'

outcomes.brand_lift_internal: +24% on 'trust authority' metric for young Saudis

what_made_it_work: Audience-trust-first framing inverts conventional government messaging
what_would_NOT_transplant: Authority must actually be ready to listen — bait-and-switch backfires
Specific to brands with credibility deficit; established trust audiences need different framing
